summary: Loader package hijacks popular dev-tool CLI names and pulls in external payload via postinstall
details: webservices.rest@99.1.3 is a loader/decoy. Its package.json declares a postinstall hook (`node index.js`) whose only effect is to `require('webservices.rest-utils')` inside a swallowed try/catch — the actual payload lives in the sibling dependency, not in this tarball. The package additionally declares `bin` entries for eleven popular developer CLI names (webpack, webpack-cli, vite, eslint, turbo, tsc, ts-node, next, jest, prettier, nodemon), all pointing at the same stub index.js; on global install these names shadow the real tools on PATH so that any subsequent invocation of those commands re-executes the loader and re-imports the payload dependency. The package name 'webservices.rest' and the inflated 99.1.3 version are consistent with a dependency-confusion / namespace-abuse attack against the OpenMRS @openmrs/* ecosystem. Installer harm: `npm install` resolves and runs attacker-controlled code in `webservices.rest-utils`, and global installs additionally trojanize the developer's everyday toolchain.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
